description: Phase 5 — Messaging Workers: extend ActionJobData with active_hours and propagate through all existing workers; send_message worker (Template Service render → Messaging Service send, active hours gating via moveToDelayed); send_email worker (Template Service render → Email Service send, active hours gating); JobCanceller service that cancels delayed BullMQ jobs on rule disable/soft-delete, wired into PATCH /rules/:id/status and DELETE /rules/:id routes; full index.ts wiring for all Phase 5 workers.

userStories.title: Add active_hours to ActionJobData and propagate through all existing workers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need active_hours to flow through every job in the action chain so that send_message and send_email workers (added in later stories) can apply active-hours gating regardless of where they appear in the chain.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify `src/queue/index.ts`: add `import type { ActiveHoursConfig } from '../services/active-hours.js'` and add `active_hours?: ActiveHoursConfig | null` field to `ActionJobData` interface

Modify `src/services/execution-manager.ts`: add `import type { ActiveHoursConfig } from './active-hours.js'`; in the `queue.add(...)` call inside `handle()`, add `active_hours: (rule.rule.active_hours as ActiveHoursConfig | null) ?? null` to the job data object
Modify `src/services/action-workers/branch.worker.ts`: in the `queue.add(...)` call for the winner step, add `active_hours: job.data.active_hours` to the job data object
Modify `src/services/action-workers/enroll-sequence.worker.ts`: in the `queue.add(...)` call for the next step, add `active_hours: job.data.active_hours` to the job data object
Modify `src/services/action-workers/emit-event.worker.ts`: in the `queue.add(...)` call for the next step, add `active_hours: job.data.active_hours` to the job data object
Update `test/unit/execution-manager.test.ts` happy-path test: assert `queue.add` was called with job data containing `active_hours: null` (since the mock rule has `active_hours: null`)
Update `test/unit/action-workers/branch-worker.test.ts`: assert `queue.add` was called with job data containing `active_hours: job.data.active_hours` value from the test fixture
Update `test/unit/action-workers/enroll-sequence-worker.test.ts`: assert `queue.add` was called with job data containing `active_hours: job.data.active_hours` value from the test fixture
Update `test/unit/action-workers/emit-event-worker.test.ts`: assert `queue.add` was called with job data containing `active_hours: job.data.active_hours` value from the test fixture
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: All five `queue.add` call sites across the four files need the `active_hours` field added. In execution-manager.ts, `rule.rule.active_hours` is typed as `unknown | null` — cast it to `ActiveHoursConfig | null`. The three existing workers just forward `job.data.active_hours` without inspection — no logic change needed in those workers, only the data forwarded. Keep test fixture `active_hours` values simple: use `null` in most tests; add one positive case (e.g., `{ start: '08:00', end: '20:00', timezone_field: 'payload.tz' }`) to the next-step enqueue assertion test to prove the value is forwarded, not hardcoded.

userStories.title: send_message worker — active hours gating + Template Service render + Messaging Service send
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a send_message action worker that checks active hours before executing, renders the message body via the Template Service, and delivers it via the Messaging Service — then enqueues the next step or marks the execution complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/action-workers/send-message.worker.ts`

Import `type { Job, Queue } from 'bullmq'`; import `type { ActionJobData } from '../../queue/index.js'`; import `type { ExecutionRepository } from '../../repositories/execution.repository.js'`; import `{ resolveParams, type ExecutionContext } from '../field-interpolator.js'`; import `{ computeDelay, type ActiveHoursConfig } from '../active-hours.js'`
Export `const TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL_ENV = 'TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL'`
Export `const MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL_ENV = 'MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL'`
Export `createSendMessageProcessor(repo: ExecutionRepository, queue: Queue<ActionJobData>, fetchFn: typeof fetch = globalThis.fetch): (job: Job<ActionJobData>) => Promise<void>`

The returned processor — step 1 (active hours check, before marking 'running'): destructure `{ execution_id, step_id, action_params, exec_ctx, event, active_hours }` from `job.data`. If `active_hours` is non-null: call `const delay = computeDelay(active_hours as ActiveHoursConfig, event as Record<string, unknown>, exec_ctx as ExecutionContext)`. If `delay > 0`: call `await job.moveToDelayed(Date.now() + delay, job.token); return;` — step stays 'pending', job re-queued at window open time
The returned processor — step 2: `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'running', { attempt: job.attemptsMade + 1 })`

The returned processor — step 3 (resolve and extract): `const resolved = resolveParams(action_params, event, exec_ctx as ExecutionContext)`. Extract: `const nextStepId = resolved['_next_step_id'] as string | undefined`, `const templateId = resolved['template_id'] as string`, `const to = resolved['to_field'] as string`, `const from = resolved['from_field'] as string`, `const context = resolved['context']`, `const dedupKey = resolved['dedup_key'] as string | undefined`. If `!dedupKey`: `console.warn('send_message: missing dedup_key for step ' + step_id)` — proceed without it

The returned processor — step 4 (Template Service render): `const templateUrl = process.env[TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL_ENV]`. If falsy: throw `new Error('TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL is not configured')`. Call `const tplRes = await fetchFn(templateUrl + '/templates/render', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ template_id: templateId, context }) })`. If `!tplRes.ok`: throw `new Error('templates/render error: ' + tplRes.status)`. Parse: `const { body_text } = await tplRes.json() as { body_text: string }`

The returned processor — step 5 (Messaging Service send): `const msgUrl = process.env[MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL_ENV]`. If falsy: throw `new Error('MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL is not configured')`. Build payload: `const msgPayload: Record<string, unknown> = { to, from, body: body_text }`. If `dedupKey`: `msgPayload['dedup_key'] = dedupKey`. Call `const msgRes = await fetchFn(msgUrl + '/messages/send', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify(msgPayload) })`. If `!msgRes.ok`: throw `new Error('messages/send error: ' + msgRes.status)`

The returned processor — step 6 (complete and chain): `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'completed', { completedAt: new Date() })`. If `nextStepId`: load `const nextStep = await repo.findStepById(nextStepId)`. If non-null: `await queue.add(nextStep.action_type, { execution_id, step_id: nextStepId, action_type: nextStep.action_type, action_params: nextStep.action_params as Record<string, unknown>, exec_ctx, event, active_hours: job.data.active_hours })`. Else (no nextStepId): `await repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'completed', new Date())`

Create `test/unit/action-workers/send-message-worker.test.ts` with mock `fetchFn: vi.fn()`, mock `repo`, mock `queue`; mock `computeDelay` via `vi.mock('../../../src/services/active-hours.js', () => ({ computeDelay: vi.fn() }))`
Test: active hours — delay returned > 0 → `job.moveToDelayed` called with `(Date.now() + delay, job.token)`, `repo.updateStepStatus('running')` NOT called, `fetchFn` NOT called
Test: active hours — delay returned === 0 → proceeds normally (no moveToDelayed)
Test: no active_hours (null) — `computeDelay` NOT called, proceeds normally

Test: happy path with next step — `fetchFn` called twice (template render then messaging send), step completed, `repo.findStepById(nextStepId)` called, `queue.add` called with correct data including `active_hours: job.data.active_hours`; `repo.updateExecutionStatus` NOT called
Test: happy path without next step — `fetchFn` called twice, step completed, `repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'completed', ...)` called; `queue.add` NOT called
Test: missing dedup_key — `console.warn` called with message containing step_id; `msgPayload` sent without `dedup_key` key; execution still completes normally
Test: TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL not set → throws Error containing 'TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL'
Test: MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL not set → throws Error containing 'MESSAGING_SERVICE_URL'
Test: template render returns non-ok status → throws Error containing status code; `repo.updateStepStatus('completed')` NOT called
Test: messages/send returns non-ok status → throws Error containing status code; `repo.updateStepStatus('completed')` NOT called
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Mock `computeDelay` from active-hours.js (not active-hours.ts) — use `.js` extension in vi.mock path. To test `job.moveToDelayed`, create `const job = { data: { ... }, token: 'test-token', attemptsMade: 0, moveToDelayed: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined) }` as the mock job. Mock `fetchFn` to return `{ ok: true, json: () => Promise.resolve({ body_text: 'Hello!' }) }` for template render and `{ ok: true }` for messaging send — use `vi.fn().mockResolvedValueOnce(...).mockResolvedValueOnce(...)` for sequencing. Set env vars in `beforeEach` and delete in `afterEach` (or use `vi.stubEnv`). The worker does NOT strip routing keys from `resolved` before constructing the HTTP bodies — it extracts specific named fields. So `_next_step_id` in `resolved` is fine; it is never included in any HTTP body.

userStories.title: send_email worker — active hours gating + Template Service render + Email Service send
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a send_email action worker that applies the same active hours gating and two-step render-then-send pattern as send_message, but delivers via the Email Service with subject, HTML body, and plain-text body.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/action-workers/send-email.worker.ts`

Import `type { Job, Queue } from 'bullmq'`; import `type { ActionJobData } from '../../queue/index.js'`; import `type { ExecutionRepository } from '../../repositories/execution.repository.js'`; import `{ resolveParams, type ExecutionContext } from '../field-interpolator.js'`; import `{ computeDelay, type ActiveHoursConfig } from '../active-hours.js'`
Export `const TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL_ENV = 'TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL'` (same env var as send_message — both use the same Template Service)
Export `const EMAIL_SERVICE_URL_ENV = 'EMAIL_SERVICE_URL'`
Export `createSendEmailProcessor(repo: ExecutionRepository, queue: Queue<ActionJobData>, fetchFn: typeof fetch = globalThis.fetch): (job: Job<ActionJobData>) => Promise<void>`

The returned processor — step 1 (active hours check, before marking 'running'): same pattern as send_message — if `active_hours` non-null and `computeDelay(...)` > 0: `await job.moveToDelayed(Date.now() + delay, job.token); return;`
The returned processor — step 2: `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'running', { attempt: job.attemptsMade + 1 })`

The returned processor — step 3 (resolve and extract): `const resolved = resolveParams(action_params, event, exec_ctx as ExecutionContext)`. Extract: `const nextStepId = resolved['_next_step_id'] as string | undefined`, `const templateId = resolved['template_id'] as string`, `const to = resolved['to_field'] as string`, `const context = resolved['context']`, `const dedupKey = resolved['dedup_key'] as string | undefined`. If `!dedupKey`: `console.warn('send_email: missing dedup_key for step ' + step_id)`

The returned processor — step 4 (Template Service render): same URL env var as send_message. Call `POST /templates/render` with `{ template_id: templateId, context }`. Expect response `{ subject: string, body_html: string, body_text: string }`. Throw on non-ok status.

The returned processor — step 5 (Email Service send): `const emailUrl = process.env[EMAIL_SERVICE_URL_ENV]`. If falsy: throw `new Error('EMAIL_SERVICE_URL is not configured')`. Build payload: `const emailPayload: Record<string, unknown> = { to, subject, body_html, body_text }`. If `dedupKey`: `emailPayload['dedup_key'] = dedupKey`. Call `POST /emails/send` with this payload. Throw on non-ok status.

The returned processor — step 6 (complete and chain): same pattern as send_message — `updateStepStatus('completed')`, enqueue next step with `active_hours: job.data.active_hours`, or `updateExecutionStatus('completed')` if no next step
Create `test/unit/action-workers/send-email-worker.test.ts` covering:
Test: active hours delay > 0 → `job.moveToDelayed` called, `repo.updateStepStatus('running')` NOT called
Test: active hours delay === 0 → proceeds normally
Test: no active_hours (null) → proceeds without calling computeDelay
Test: happy path with next step — two fetch calls (template render + email send), step completed, queue.add called with `active_hours: job.data.active_hours`; updateExecutionStatus NOT called
Test: happy path without next step — step completed, `updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'completed', ...)` called
Test: missing dedup_key — `console.warn` called; email payload sent without dedup_key; execution still completes
Test: TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL not set → throws containing 'TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL'
Test: EMAIL_SERVICE_URL not set → throws containing 'EMAIL_SERVICE_URL'
Test: template render non-ok → throws containing status; step NOT completed
Test: email send non-ok → throws containing status; step NOT completed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
send_email has no `from_field` param — Email Service manages the from address. Template render response for email has three fields (`subject`, `body_html`, `body_text`) vs one for SMS (`body_text`). Mock `fetchFn` with two `mockResolvedValueOnce` calls: first returns `{ ok: true, json: () => Promise.resolve({ subject: 'Hi', body_html: '

Hi

', body_text: 'Hi' }) }`, second returns `{ ok: true }`. Use `vi.mock` on `'../../../src/services/active-hours.js'` for `computeDelay` — same pattern as send_message tests. `TEMPLATE_SERVICE_URL_ENV` is re-exported from this file (not re-imported from send-message.worker.ts) — it's a simple string constant that can be duplicated.

userStories.title: JobCanceller service + route wiring + index.ts wiring for Phase 5 workers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a JobCanceller service that removes delayed BullMQ jobs when a rule is disabled or soft-deleted, wired into the rules routes and into index.ts alongside the send_message and send_email workers — completing Phase 5 end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/job-canceller.ts`
Import `type { Queue } from 'bullmq'`; import `type { ActionJobData } from '../queue/index.js'`

Export `class JobCanceller { constructor(private readonly queue: Queue<ActionJobData>) {} async cancelDelayedJobsForRule(ruleId: string): Promise<number> { const delayed = await this.queue.getDelayed(); const toCancel = delayed.filter((j) => j.data.exec_ctx.rule_id === ruleId); await Promise.all(toCancel.map((j) => j.remove())); return toCancel.length; } }`
Modify `src/routes/rules.ts`: add `import type { JobCanceller } from '../services/job-canceller.js'`; change plugin options type from `{ db: Knex }` to `{ db: Knex; jobCanceller?: JobCanceller }`

In `PATCH /rules/:id/status` handler: after `repo.updateStatus(id, status)` returns a non-null rule, if `status === 'disabled'`: `await opts.jobCanceller?.cancelDelayedJobsForRule(id)` (before sending the response)
In `DELETE /rules/:id` handler: after `repo.softDelete(id)` returns true: `await opts.jobCanceller?.cancelDelayedJobsForRule(id)` (before sending the 204 response)

Modify `src/index.ts`: import `{ createSendMessageProcessor } from './services/action-workers/send-message.worker.js'`; import `{ createSendEmailProcessor } from './services/action-workers/send-email.worker.js'`; import `{ JobCanceller } from './services/job-canceller.js'`
In `src/index.ts`: after `const queue = createQueue(connection)`, add `const jobCanceller = new JobCanceller(queue)`
In `src/index.ts`: add `createSendMessageProcessor` and `createSendEmailProcessor` workers to the `workers` array using `createActionWorker`
In `src/index.ts`: update the `fastify.register(rulesRoutes, { db })` call to `fastify.register(rulesRoutes, { db, jobCanceller })`

Create `test/unit/job-canceller.test.ts`: mock `Queue` with `getDelayed: vi.fn()` returning an array of mock jobs each having `data: { exec_ctx: { rule_id: string }, ... }` and `remove: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)`
Test: `cancelDelayedJobsForRule('rule-1')` with two matching and two non-matching delayed jobs — only matching jobs have `remove()` called; returns 2
Test: no matching delayed jobs — no `remove()` calls; returns 0
Test: empty delayed queue — returns 0 immediately
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The optional chaining `opts.jobCanceller?.cancelDelayedJobsForRule(id)` keeps backward compatibility for tests that don't pass a jobCanceller. In index.ts, `jobCanceller` must be created AFTER `const queue = createQueue(connection)` but BEFORE `fastify.register(rulesRoutes, ...)`. However, the current index.ts creates the queue AFTER `fastify.listen(...)` while `rulesRoutes` is registered BEFORE listen. This means `jobCanceller` cannot be constructed at register time with the current code structure. The fix: move `const connection = ...` and `const queue = createQueue(connection)` and `const jobCanceller = new JobCanceller(queue)` to BEFORE `fastify.register(rulesRoutes, ...)` — ahead of the listen call. Then pass `{ db, jobCanceller }` at register time. The workers array (which also uses `queue`) is created after listen in the current code — keep workers there but create queue/jobCanceller earlier. Double-check SIGTERM handler still closes all workers. For JobCanceller tests: `vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)` on `queue.getDelayed` returning an array literal of mock job objects is sufficient — no need to import real Job class.
